description:
You are a data engineer working on an ETL pipeline.  
    Your source data currently lives in the directory  
        /home/user/etl_data  
    and you want to make a **local** backup copy under  
        /home/user/backup/etl_data  

    Goal  
    -----  
    1. Synchronise everything that is inside /home/user/etl_data into the directory /home/user/backup/etl_data.  
       • If /home/user/backup or /home/user/backup/etl_data do not exist, create them automatically as part of the
         same command.  
       • Preserve timestamps and file permissions.  
    2. Immediately after the synchronisation finishes, create a plain-text log file named  
           /home/user/etl_sync.log  
       that contains an **alphabetically-sorted** list of the filenames that were synchronised, **one filename per line,
       with no directory paths, timestamps, headers, or extra text**.  
       Example of the required format (your list must exactly match the actual files that end up in the backup directory):  
           customers.csv  
           orders.csv  
           readme.txt  

    Constraints  
    -----------  
    • Accomplish both steps (sync + log creation) with a single terminal command line (command chaining with && or a
      subshell is acceptable; do not write a shell script file).  
    • Do not use sudo or any other privilege-escalation tool.  
    • The automated test will check two things:  
        1. Every file that exists under /home/user/etl_data at the moment the command is run now also exists under
           /home/user/backup/etl_data with identical content.  
        2. /home/user/etl_sync.log exists and its contents are exactly the alphabetically-sorted bare filenames
           (no paths) of the files that were copied, one per line, no extra whitespace or blank lines.  

    Hints  
    -----  
    • Any standards-compliant file-synchronisation tool available on a minimal Linux system (e.g. rsync, cp, install)
      is acceptable, provided the command meets all requirements above.  
    • Remember to capture or generate the filename list **after** the copy completes so the log truly reflects what
      ended up in the backup directory.
truth:
Initial setup BEFORE the agent runs the command  
    -----------------------------------------------  
    The directory /home/user/etl_data already exists and contains exactly these three files:  

    1. /home/user/etl_data/customers.csv  
       Contents (including trailing newline):  
           id,name
           1,Alice
           2,Bob

    2. /home/user/etl_data/orders.csv  
       Contents (including trailing newline):  
           order_id,customer_id,amount
           1001,1,250.00
           1002,2,150.00

    3. /home/user/etl_data/readme.txt  
       Contents (including trailing newline):  
           Sample ETL source data.

    Nothing else exists under /home/user/etl_data (no sub-directories).  
    The directory /home/user/backup does NOT exist yet.  
    The file /home/user/etl_sync.log does NOT exist yet.  

    Desired state AFTER successful task completion  
    ----------------------------------------------  
    1. The directory tree /home/user/backup/etl_data now exists and contains **byte-for-byte identical** copies of the
       three files listed above.  
    2. /home/user/etl_sync.log exists and its exact contents are:  
           customers.csv
           orders.csv
           readme.txt
       (Each line ends with a single UNIX newline character `\n`. No extra blank lines before or after.)
name: task_000000_6c34b33c
